message:
test(golden): teach capture_robot_json about commands whose non-zero exit is part of the contract

`cass health --json` is the second golden in this harness whose
non-zero exit status is a contract, not an error: health exits 1 when
the DB / index is not initialised, and the JSON body that accompanies
that exit is precisely what we want to freeze. The initial landing of
the health golden unconditionally asserted `status.success()`, which
would cause the test to panic before the scrub/compare step ever ran.

- Extend `capture_robot_json` with an `expect_status: ExpectStatus`
  parameter:
    * `ExpectStatus::ExitOk` — the command must exit zero. Used by
      `capabilities_json_matches_golden` and
      `models_status_json_matches_golden`.
    * `ExpectStatus::ExitAny` — exit status is part of the contract;
      proceed regardless. Used by `health_json_matches_golden`.
  The success branch keeps the existing rich panic message (status
  + stdout + stderr) so the next golden-introducer still gets a
  useful failure when a command regresses into hard failure.
- Migrate the existing two goldens to the new call shape and add the
  `ExitAny` plumbing to the health golden. Doc comment on
  `capture_robot_json` explains why both modes exist so the next
  reader doesn't "simplify" the split away.
- `.beads/issues.jsonl` + `.beads/last-touched` bump from br sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.4","title":"Add end-to-end search latency benchmark proving sub-60ms claim","description":"WHAT: Create an end-to-end search latency benchmark that proves the README claim of sub-60ms search-as-you-type latency.

CURRENT STATE:
- Component benchmarks exist in benches/search_perf.rs with targets: hash embedding <1ms, RRF <5ms, vector search 10k <5ms, vector search 50k <20ms
- No single benchmark measures the complete path: keystroke -> query parse -> cache check -> Bloom gate -> Tantivy search -> result formatting -> return
- The claim is CREDIBLE given the architecture (LRU cache + Bloom64 prefix gates + edge n-grams) but UNPROVEN

WHAT THE BENCHMARK SHOULD MEASURE:
1. Cold search (no cache): query string -> SearchResult vector, measuring wall clock time
2. Warm search (cache populated): same query repeated, measuring cache hit path
3. Prefix search (simulating typing): 'a' -> 'au' -> 'aut' -> 'auth', measuring each increment
4. Search with filters (agent, workspace, date range)
5. Search on corpus of realistic size (1K, 10K, 50K messages)

BENCHMARK DESIGN:
- Use Criterion.rs (already a dev-dependency)
- Create a fixture with synthetic but realistic conversation data at multiple scales
- Measure p50, p95, p99 latencies
- Assert p95 < 60ms for corpus sizes up to 50K messages on warm path
- Record results in a way that CI can track regressions

KEY FILES:
- benches/search_latency_e2e.rs (new benchmark file)
- benches/search_perf.rs (existing component benchmarks for reference)
- src/search/query.rs (the search execution path being measured)

DONE WHEN:
- cargo bench --bench search_latency_e2e runs successfully
- p95 warm-path latency < 60ms for 50K message corpus
- Results are documented in benchmark output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:48:55.307843033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:38:53.732702723Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:38:53.732020276Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarks","performance","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:48:55.307843033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":560,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"TESTING AND BENCHMARK REQUIREMENTS:
Create benches/search_latency_e2e.rs with Criterion groups:
1. group: cold_search (first query after index open)
   - Measure: query parse + index open + Tantivy search + result format
   - Corpus: 1K, 10K, 50K messages
   - Assert: p95 < 200ms for 50K cold
2. group: warm_search (repeated query, cache populated)
   - Measure: cache check + Bloom gate + return cached
   - Assert: p95 < 5ms for cache hit
3. group: prefix_typing (simulating keystrokes: a -> au -> aut -> auth)
   - Measure: each incremental prefix query
   - Assert: p95 < 60ms per keystroke for 50K corpus
4. group: filtered_search (with agent + workspace + date filters)
   - Measure: filter application overhead
   - Assert: < 2x unfiltered latency
5. Fixture: Use synthetic conversations via FrankenStorage::insert_conversations_batched
6. Logging: Each benchmark prints corpus size, hit count, cache state, and wall clock time
7. CI integration: Add to benches/ Cargo.toml section, runnable via cargo bench --bench search_latency_e2e","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:49:31Z"},{"id":567,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVISION 2 (refinement round 2):

IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: A new bench requires a [[bench]] entry in Cargo.toml:
[[bench]]
name = "search_latency_e2e"
harness = false

All existing benches use harness = false (Criterion.rs). Follow the same pattern.

ALTERNATIVELY: Could add the E2E benchmark groups to the existing benches/search_perf.rs instead of a new file. This avoids creating a new file (AGENTS.md prefers editing existing files). The existing search_perf.rs already has 20 benchmarks and adding 4 more Criterion groups is clean.

RECOMMENDATION: Add to search_perf.rs rather than creating search_latency_e2e.rs. Group the new benchmarks under a bench_group! called 'e2e_latency' to keep them organized.","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.5","title":"Implement message-level noise filtering (unimplemented README feature)","description":"WHAT: Verify that message-level noise filtering (empty messages, whitespace-only, repeated tool acknowledgments) is complete and tested.

CURRENT STATE:
- Watch-level noise filtering confirmed: indexer/mod.rs line 4856 ignores remove events as noise
- Tool-result secret redaction confirmed: indexer/mod.rs line 5833
- Multiple is_empty() checks throughout indexing code
- Message-level filtering LIKELY complete in franken_agent_detection (FAD) connector layer
- BUT: no cass-level integration test explicitly verifies noise messages are excluded from search results

THE README CLAIMS:
'Common low-value content is filtered from results: Empty messages, Pure whitespace, System prompts (unless searching for them), Repeated tool acknowledgments'

APPROACH:
1. Trace the full message path: FAD connector.scan() -> storage insert -> Tantivy indexing
2. Identify WHERE each noise type is filtered (FAD? storage? indexer?)
3. For each noise type, either:
   a) Confirm it IS filtered with a specific code path reference, OR
   b) Add the filter if missing
4. Create an integration test that:
   - Feeds conversations with noise messages into the indexer
   - Searches for content that would match noise
   - Asserts noise messages are NOT in search results
   - Asserts legitimate messages ARE in search results

KEY FILES:
- src/indexer/mod.rs (indexing pipeline)
- src/connectors/ (re-exports from FAD, trace into FAD if needed)
- tests/ (new or augmented test file)

DONE WHEN:
- Each noise type (empty, whitespace, tool ack, system prompt) has a documented filter location
- An integration test proves all 4 noise types are excluded from search results
- The test passes: cargo test noise_filter (or similar)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:11.621295543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:39:48.992936070Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:39:48.992189652Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","indexer","noise-filtering","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:11.621295543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":559,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENTS:
Create tests/noise_filtering_integration.rs with:
1. test_empty_messages_excluded_from_search - Index conversation with empty messages, verify search skips them
2. test_whitespace_only_excluded - Messages with only spaces/tabs/newlines are filtered
3. test_repeated_tool_acks_deduplicated - Repeated 'Tool result: ok' messages collapsed or filtered
4. test_system_prompts_excluded_by_default - System role messages not in results unless explicitly searched
5. test_system_prompts_included_when_searched - Searching for system prompt content DOES return them
6. test_legitimate_short_messages_preserved - Short but meaningful messages like 'yes' or 'done' are NOT filtered
7. All tests use FrankenStorage + frankensearch index for full pipeline coverage
8. Each test logs: total messages ingested, messages indexed, messages in search results","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:49:30Z"},{"id":568,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVISION 2 (refinement round 2):

CRITICAL FINDING: No explicit noise filtering code found ANYWHERE in cass or FAD.

Searched for: is_empty, trim().is_empty, skip_empty, filter_noise, noise_filter, skip_message, empty_content, filter_message — zero matches in both repos.

The README section 'Noise Filtering' (lines 1459-1466) claims:
- Empty messages are filtered
- Pure whitespace is filtered
- System prompts are filtered (unless searching for them)
- Repeated tool acknowledgments are filtered

THIS IS AN UNIMPLEMENTED FEATURE, not a partially implemented one.

REVISED SCOPE: This bead should IMPLEMENT noise filtering, not just audit it.

APPROACH:
1. Add a message filter in the indexing pipeline (src/indexer/mod.rs) that runs BEFORE Tantivy indexing
2. Filter criteria:
   a) content.trim().is_empty() — skip empty/whitespace messages
   b) role == System AND not in explicit system search — skip system prompts
   c) Content dedup within conversation — collapse repeated tool ack messages
3. Make filtering configurable (env var CASS_SKIP_NOISE_FILTER=1 to disable)
4. Log filtered message counts at debug level

TESTING: Create tests/noise_filtering_integration.rs (justified since this is genuinely new functionality — tests/ is the standard location):
- 7 test functions as previously specified
- Use FrankenStorage + frankensearch for full pipeline
- Each test logs ingested vs indexed message counts

DONE WHEN: noise filtering works and is tested, or README is updated to remove the claim","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","title":"Achieve full test suite green gate (all test files compile and pass)","description":"WHAT: Ensure ALL 122 test files in tests/ compile and pass, establishing a green CI gate.

CURRENT STATE:
- 122 .rs files in tests/ directory
- 12 tests in e2e_pages.rs are known to fail (covered by sibling bead 3e3qg.3)
- Some tests may have been broken by recent frankensqlite changes
- The frankensqlite dependency occasionally introduces compile errors via path dep

THIS BEAD IS THE FINAL GATE:
After all other beads in this epic are complete, run the full test suite and fix any remaining failures. This is the integration verification step.

APPROACH:
1. Run cargo test --all-targets 2>&1 and capture full output
2. Categorize failures: compile errors, runtime panics, assertion failures, timeouts
3. Fix each failure (or file it as a separate bead if complex)
4. Verify cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
5. Verify cargo fmt --check passes

DONE WHEN:
- cargo test --all-targets passes with 0 failures
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- cargo fmt --check passes
- This can be run as a CI gate","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:23.412439416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:07:32.786587858Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","quality-gate","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:23.412439416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:50:59.378587257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:04.014001857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:48:56.497220441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:31:14.579804309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:31:46.995090734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:00.459661430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:01.124671566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:02.078094400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:02.769285083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:03.346870740Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":625,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","author":"StormyOwl","text":"2026-04-22 validation slice after switching off stuck rch full-suite run: local env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_cass_shared cargo check --tests passed; cargo test --tests --no-run passed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --check passed; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo test --test e2e_pages -- --nocapture passed 67/67. No current test compilation failures found. Full runtime suite was not rerun per operator instruction to avoid whole-suite rch waits; remaining work is runtime sharding/module-by-module execution, not a compile fix.","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:07:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","title":"Update README.md to accurately reflect current feature state","description":"WHAT: Update README.md to accurately reflect what the code actually delivers.

CURRENT DISCREPANCIES (minor, mostly understated):
1. README says '6 theme presets' but code has 19 presets (Dark, Light, Catppuccin, Dracula, Nord, SolarizedDark, SolarizedLight, Monokai, GruvboxDark, OneDark, RosePine, Everforest, Kanagawa, AyuMirage, Nightfox, CyberpunkAurora, Synthwave84, HighContrast, Colorblind). Update to reflect real count.

2. README lists 13 main connectors but code has 18 (missing: copilot_cli, crush, kimi, qwen from README listing). Update connector list.

3. README says 'SHA-256 content-based dedup' but code uses blake3. Update to say blake3.

4. README mentions 'Doctor command' which DOES exist (lib.rs:505) but the doctor sub-section could be more prominent since it has --fix and --force-rebuild modes.

5. After rusqlite migration completes, update the Architecture section to remove references to dual rusqlite/frankensqlite storage.

APPROACH:
- Do NOT rewrite the README - just fix the specific discrepancies
- Keep the existing structure and tone
- Update version numbers if applicable

DONE WHEN:
- Theme count updated to 19
- Connector list reflects all 18 connectors
- Dedup algorithm says blake3 not SHA-256
- No stale references to rusqlite in architecture section (post-migration)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:37.700981916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T18:47:58.551014373Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","readme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:37.700981916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:05.528730303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":556,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"NOTE: bv suggested this may overlap with ibuuh.12 (docs update for new search contract). These are complementary not duplicative: .7 fixes current inaccuracies (theme count, connector list, blake3 vs SHA-256), while ibuuh.12 documents the NEW hybrid search contract. Coordinate but dont block on each other.","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:47:58Z"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","title":"Update README.md to accurately reflect current feature state","description":"WHAT: Update README.md to accurately reflect what the code actually delivers.

CURRENT DISCREPANCIES (minor, mostly understated):
1. README says '6 theme presets' but code has 19 presets (Dark, Light, Catppuccin, Dracula, Nord, SolarizedDark, SolarizedLight, Monokai, GruvboxDark, OneDark, RosePine, Everforest, Kanagawa, AyuMirage, Nightfox, CyberpunkAurora, Synthwave84, HighContrast, Colorblind). Update to reflect real count.

2. README lists 13 main connectors but code has 18 (missing: copilot_cli, crush, kimi, qwen from README listing). Update connector list.

3. README says 'SHA-256 content-based dedup' but code uses blake3. Update to say blake3.

4. README mentions 'Doctor command' which DOES exist (lib.rs:505) but the doctor sub-section could be more prominent since it has --fix and --force-rebuild modes.

5. After rusqlite migration completes, update the Architecture section to remove references to dual rusqlite/frankensqlite storage.

APPROACH:
- Do NOT rewrite the README - just fix the specific discrepancies
- Keep the existing structure and tone
- Update version numbers if applicable

DONE WHEN:
- Theme count updated to 19
- Connector list reflects all 18 connectors
- Dedup algorithm says blake3 not SHA-256
- No stale references to rusqlite in architecture section (post-migration)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:37.700981916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:15:49.713218008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","readme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:37.700981916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:51:05.528730303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":556,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"NOTE: bv suggested this may overlap with ibuuh.12 (docs update for new search contract). These are complementary not duplicative: .7 fixes current inaccuracies (theme count, connector list, blake3 vs SHA-256), while ibuuh.12 documents the NEW hybrid search contract. Coordinate but dont block on each other.","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:47:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.8","title":"Add two-tier progressive search integration test","description":"WHAT: Add an integration test proving the two-tier progressive search path works end-to-end.

CURRENT STATE:
- Two-tier search code exists in src/search/two_tier_search.rs with full implementation
- CLI flags are wired: --two-tier, --fast-only, --quality-only (lib.rs lines 371-386)
- Flags correctly map to SemanticTierMode::Progressive/FastOnly/QualityOnly (lib.rs line 2969-2977)
- SemanticSearchOptions carries tier_mode into run_cli_search
- BUT: tests/e2e_two_tier_search.rs exists - need to verify it actually exercises the full path
- The two-tier path depends on: (a) hash embedder (always available), (b) daemon for quality tier

WHAT THE TEST SHOULD VERIFY:
1. --fast-only returns results using only the hash embedder (no daemon needed)
2. --quality-only attempts daemon connection and falls back gracefully if unavailable
3. --two-tier returns fast results first, then refines (or falls back to fast-only)
4. Verify the SemanticTierMode enum is correctly propagated through the search pipeline
5. Verify that hybrid mode with two-tier enabled produces valid RRF-fused results

APPROACH:
1. Review existing tests/e2e_two_tier_search.rs for coverage
2. Fill gaps with new test cases using the hash embedder (zero external deps)
3. Use --robot --json output for assertion parsing
4. Test graceful degradation when daemon is unavailable

KEY FILES:
- src/search/two_tier_search.rs (implementation)
- src/search/query.rs (search execution with tier_mode)
- tests/e2e_two_tier_search.rs (existing tests to augment)

DONE WHEN:
- cargo test --test e2e_two_tier_search passes with tests covering all 3 tier modes
- Graceful fallback verified when daemon unavailable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:55.065334907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:47:08.501243303Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:47:08.500993055Z","close_reason":"All 65 e2e_two_tier_search tests pass (0 failures, 0.06s). The 14 search tests plus 51 util tests all green. No new tests needed. Verified: cargo test --test e2e_two_tier_search → 65 passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["search","semantic","tests","two-tier"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.8","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T17:49:55.065334907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":557,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVISION (April 6 2026 refinement pass):

CORRECTION: tests/e2e_two_tier_search.rs ALREADY contains 14 comprehensive tests:
- 3 correctness tests (fast/quality/progressive ground truth)
- 3 latency tests (fast <5ms, quality <20ms, progressive timing)  
- 3 scoring tests (normalization, blending, weight effects)
- 3 edge case tests (empty index, k > docs, different queries)
- 1 robustness test (graceful degradation without daemon)
- 1 performance test (fast_only skips refinement)

REVISED SCOPE: This bead should focus on RUNNING these tests and verifying they pass, not writing new ones. If they all pass, close immediately. If any fail, diagnose and fix. Only write NEW tests if the existing 14 leave a gap (unlikely given the coverage).

PRIORITY DOWNGRADE CANDIDATE: Given the tests already exist, this might just be cargo test --test e2e_two_tier_search. If it passes, this is a 5-minute bead.","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:48:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3e3qg.9","title":"Verify FrankenStorage has full SqliteStorage API parity before rusqlite removal","description":"WHAT: Before removing rusqlite from storage/sqlite.rs, verify that FrankenStorage covers every public method that SqliteStorage exposes.

CONTEXT:
Memory from March 2026 session says 'FrankenStorage now has full method parity with SqliteStorage (database_path, insert_conversations_batched, open_or_rebuild, etc.)'. This bead verifies that claim is still true and comprehensive.

APPROACH:
1. Extract the complete public API surface of SqliteStorage (all pub fn methods)
2. Extract the complete public API surface of FrankenStorage
3. Diff the two: any method in SqliteStorage not in FrankenStorage is a gap
4. For each gap: either add to FrankenStorage or confirm it is intentionally omitted
5. Run the parity test: cargo test --test storage_frankensqlite_parity

SPECIAL ATTENTION:
- FTS5 operations (rebuild, shadow table repair)
- Transaction semantics (begin/commit/rollback)
- Migration paths (schema_version checks, migration SQL)
- Batch operations (insert_conversations_batched)
- Analytics table lifecycle
- Connection pooling (LazyFrankenDb, SendFrankenConnection)

KEY FILES:
- src/storage/sqlite.rs (both SqliteStorage and FrankenStorage defined here)
- tests/storage_frankensqlite_parity.rs (parity test suite)

DONE WHEN:
- Every pub fn on SqliteStorage has a corresponding pub fn on FrankenStorage (or documented reason for omission)
- cargo test --test storage_frankensqlite_parity passes
